rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Accept Auction Bid For Judgment Debtor Tina Yan'S Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2024 line 1. CLAIMANT BRYANT FU, PAUL LIANG NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO ACCEPT AUCTION BID FOR JUDGMENT DEBTOR TINA YAN'S DWELLING is OFF CALENDAR. Premature filing of the motion by non-parties. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Motion For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2024 line 1. INTERVENOR BRYANT FU, PAUL LIANG JOINT MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE is DENIED without prejudice. The purported purpose for the intervention (seeking approval of the Sheriff's Sale) is not supported by authority. The Court notes that the companion motion to accept auction bid for judgment debtor Tina Yan's dwelling contained no evidence that would allow the acceptance of the lower bid, i.e. evidence regarding the sufficiency of the bid to cover Li's judgment, outstanding senior debts and the homestead, let alone any additional funds to be disbursed to Fu. Furthermore, there is no authority whatsoever cited for the proposition that the bidder has standing to seek the relief of bid acceptance. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA DARK ET AL VS. EMILIE M CALHOUN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt; Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT FIDUCIARY LAW ATTORNEYS, EMILIE CALHOUN AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALT; MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its substantive and procedural burden on the Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication but not timely filing and serving a required Separate Statement and evidence upon which this motion is based. If the Court were to consider the late filings, the motion would be DENIED as there are triable issues of fact regarding each cause of action asserted in the complaint (based on ownership and $100K loan issues). See Additional Fact #70; Facts 49, 53, 60. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TEMPLE VIEW CAPITAL FUNDING, LP VS. TONY GARNICKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF TEMPLE VIEW CAPITAL FUNDING, LP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is DENIED. Triable issue of material fact exists as to the calculation of deficiency balance. Plaintiff's Evidentiary Objections are SUSTAINED notwithstanding Plaintiff's untimely filing of the Amended Declaration of Justin Wenk, the Amended Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment, or, in the alternative, Summary Adjudication, and the Amended Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SHUKU YASUDA ET AL VS. DALTON SIONG LIK HO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY, FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege facts sufficient to support the following causes of action: 1) Declaratory relief - Plaintiffs do not allege facts to support any derivative causes of action; 2) Quiet Title - Plaintiffs do not allege facts that they seek interest in the real property, only that they seek monetary judgment; 3) Fraudulent transfers - Plaintiffs do not allege facts of any transfers; 4) Negligence - Plaintiffs do not allege facts to establish any duty of care owed by Defendants; 5) Conspiracy to defraud - Plaintiffs do not allege any facts for another tort(s). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
